competitors.name: You
competitors.rules: Your real Robinhood account, rebased to the shared origin so it's comparable. Performance only — holdings and trades are deliberately not published.

competitors.name: congress_mirror
competitors.rules: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

competitors.name: momentum_breakout
competitors.rules: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume
fill to cap: breakout > 20d high on volume

competitors.name: blended_momo_rsi
competitors.rules: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.

trade_log.reason: fill to cap: breakout, RSI 64 not overbought
fill to cap: breakout, RSI 66 not overbought

competitors.name: Robinhood
competitors.rules: Real Robinhood Agentic cash account (••••) running the funded algorithms (100% momentum_breakout) live: each run-robin reruns their signals on the latest bar and trades the real account — buy fresh breakouts, exit on the strategy's sell rule.
competitors.note: Real money — the actual Robinhood Agentic account (••••), not paper. Dollar/share figures are scaled ×100 to the $10,000 display notional like the rest of the board (the live account is ~$100). Allocation: 100% momentum_breakout. Change it in state/robin_alloc.json or just ask.

competitors.name: S&P 500
competitors.rules: Buys SPY with the whole book on the first forward session and holds — the market baseline.

trade_log.reason: buy & hold: all-in on day one

competitors.name: mean_reversion
competitors.rules: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.

competitors.name: llm_voters
competitors.rules: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.

competitors.name: deep_research_analyst
competitors.rules: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).

competitors.name: mirofish_real
competitors.rules: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.

analyst.framework: Claude for Financial Services — equity-research methodology (/screen, /sector, /comps, /catalysts, /thesis). Data: Robinhood fundamentals + web_search (no paid-vendor MCP connectors).
analyst.action: Rotate the defensive/value sleeve into a semis-led leadership book: exit JPM and GE, cut UNH, keep BAC as the lone cyclical diversifier, add MRVL/TSM and a LLY growth hedge; lift deployment to ~62% with cash as the only downside hedge.
analyst.sizing: 5 names, ~62% invested / 38% cash. MU 0.16, MRVL 0.15, TSM 0.13, LLY 0.10, BAC 0.08. Semis ~44% of book (MU/MRVL/TSM), so cash is the explicit hedge against a sector-correlated drawdown. All orders market-on-open (no limits) — the prior-tick lesson was under-participating in leadership, so prioritize fills over a cuter entry.

regime.label: Risk-on; Fed on hold with cuts priced; AI-semis leadership; energy de-escalating
regime.note: Quarter is closing as the best for the S&P 500/Nasdaq in six years. Fed held at 3.50-3.75% with the market pricing two 2026 cuts. The Iran war is winding down (peace talks set to resume 6/30), pulling oil lower and relieving cyclicals on growth hopes; AI semis are the clear leadership (6/29 Nasdaq-100 +2.3%). Tilt: own the semis leaders with room on the RSI, keep one cheap financial for the de-escalation rotation, hold a non-cyclical growth hedge (LLY), and let cash — not a defensive stock — be the downside hedge.

analyst.thesis: What changed since 6/29: the risk-on inflection I flagged last tick is now confirmed by the tape, so I'm committing capital to it instead of starting it. The energy/Iran shock is de-escalating (oil falling, peace talks resuming), the Fed is on hold with two cuts priced, and AI semiconductors are unambiguous market leadership (6/29 Nasdaq-100 +2.3%; Astera +16%, KLA +12%, AMAT +11%). My relative-strength screen across the universe puts semis at the top (MRVL +35.7% / MU +11.2% / TSM +9.6% over 20d vs SPY -1.6%) while energy is the worst sleeve (SLB -15%, OXY -18%, RSI 13-25) — the same rotation. So I rotate the financials/industrials value sleeve (exit JPM, exit GE, cut the failed UNH hedge) into a semis-led book anchored on the best risk/reward, not the most extended: MU (cheapest semis P/E at 25.9, memory/HBM super-cycle, pulled back -8% from its high), MRVL (the custom-AI-silicon leader, but RSI only 57 with a -10% pullback = room), and TSM (foundry monopoly at a reasonable 37.8x into 7/16 earnings). LLY adds non-cyclical GLP-1 growth that doesn't move with semis, and BAC stays as one cheap (14.4x) financial that benefits from the de-escalation/growth rotation into its 7/14 earnings. I keep 38% cash because the book is semis-concentrated and I'm down 10.6% all-time — cash, not a bleeding defensive name, is the hedge.

evidence.point: S&P 500 is closing 'the best quarter in years'; on 6/29 the S&P rose 1.2%, the Nasdaq-100 surged 2.3%, and the Dow gained 307 pts to a fresh record high.
Fed held at 3.50%-3.75% with investors continuing to price in two cuts for 2026.
'The potential wind down of the Iran war' with 'falling oil prices helping relieve pressure on more cyclical sectors like industrials and financials'; Trump says peace talks resume Tuesday.
Semis led: the VanEck Semiconductor ETF gained more than 3%, with Astera Labs ~+16%, KLA ~+12%, and Applied Materials ~+11% (6/29).
'Micron's rally has room to run as the AI semiconductor cycle continues to reshape the memory business'; MU rose 10% on 6/15 on Iran de-escalation + AI chip-demand reports.
MU P/E 25.93, 52-wk high 1255.00 (6/25), last 1151 (-8.3% from high), RSI 61 — cheapest semis multiple in the finalist set.

Marvell 'has significantly benefited from large cloud companies designing custom AI accelerators, particularly in networking and optical interconnect.' MRVL P/E 94.9, 52-wk high 329.88 (6/18), today ranged 275.50-300.00, RSI 57, +35.7% over 20d.

'Taiwan Semiconductor said the demand for AI chips is growing significantly, with customers giving a very positive outlook.' TSM P/E 37.8, set a 52-wk high of 479.00 on 6/30; Q2 earnings 2026-07-16, est EPS 3.77.
LLY P/E 44.3, 52-wk high 1238.00 (6/29), RSI 61 — non-cyclical GLP-1 growth uncorrelated to semis.
BAC P/E 14.36, dividend yield 1.9%, RSI 68; Q2 earnings 2026-07-14 (am), est EPS 1.10 — cheap financial leveraged to the de-escalation/growth rotation.

risks: Semis concentration (~44% of book in MU/MRVL/TSM): a sector-wide selloff like 6/4-6/5 (AMD/INTC down hard, MU -7%) hits all three together; cash (38%) and the 0.20 per-name stop are the only ballast.
Chasing extended leadership: TSM (52-wk high), and the group has already run a full quarter — a momentum reversal or profit-taking into quarter-end/start could whipsaw fresh entries.
TSM 7/16 earnings is binary: a soft AI/foundry print or weak guide would re-rate the whole semis sleeve, not just TSM.
Re-escalation risk: if Iran peace talks collapse, oil spikes back, cyclicals/risk reverse, and the de-escalation thesis underpinning the rotation breaks.
ASML-style equipment-order worries (semicap fell 5-6% on order concerns earlier in June) could spread to the broader semis complex.

data_examined.label: Universe relative-strength / momentum / RSI screen across all 100 names off the 2026-06-30 snapshot (SPY 20d -1.6%)
Robinhood fundamentals (P/E, 52-wk range, market cap, dividend) on 10 finalists: MRVL/ASML/AMD/MU/TSM/INTC/LLY/GE/BAC/CAT
Robinhood earnings calendar, next 31 days, high-cap: BAC 7/14, ASML 7/15, TSM 7/16, GE 7/16, INTC 7/23
Macro regime + semis demand read (Fed path, Iran de-escalation, oil, semis leadership, Deloitte $975B 2026 chip-sales outlook)

rationale.MU: Anchor and highest conviction. [DATA] Cheapest semis multiple in the set (P/E 25.9 vs MRVL 95 / AMD 177), riding the AI memory/HBM super-cycle that analysts say 'has room to run.' [DATA] It pulled back -8.3% from its 6/25 52-wk high (1255) to ~1151 with RSI at 61 — a better entry than the extended leaders. [ASSUMED] I already hold it (+1.5% vs entry) and not panic-selling the 6/26 -7.5% scare was validated; I'm bumping it to the top weight. Catalyst: continued AI-memory demand reports; ex-dividend 7/6.
rationale.MRVL: The cleanest leadership risk/reward. [DATA] Strongest 20-day relative strength in the universe (+35.7% vs SPY -1.6%) yet RSI is only 57 and it sits -9.7% below its 6/18 high (329.88) — i.e. a pullback in the leader, not a blow-off top; today it reclaimed $300 after dipping to 275.50. [DATA] Structurally it's the custom-AI-silicon / optical-interconnect winner as hyperscalers design their own accelerators. [ASSUMED] New position — the highest-momentum name I can still enter without chasing an overbought RSI. Next earnings >31 days out, so this is a thematic AI-capex call, not an earnings bet.
rationale.TSM: Quality foundry anchor. [DATA] Monopoly on leading-edge nodes at a reasonable 37.8x; management says AI-chip demand is 'growing significantly' with a 'very positive' customer outlook; printed a 52-wk high (479.00) on 6/30, RSI 62. [DATA] Q2 earnings 7/16 (est EPS 3.77, ~12 ticks out) is both the catalyst and the main risk — a strong print leads the group. [ASSUMED] Sized 0.13 (below MU/MRVL) precisely because it's at a high into a binary earnings event.
rationale.LLY: Non-cyclical diversifier. [DATA] GLP-1 franchise growth that does not co-move with semis; P/E 44.3, RSI 61, near its 6/29 52-wk high (1238). [ASSUMED] This is the book's ballast against a semis-correlated drawdown — growth exposure that isn't rate- or chip-cycle-sensitive. Carried over from last tick's target list where I'd flagged adding it.
rationale.BAC: The one cyclical kept. [DATA] Cheapest finalist at 14.4x with a 1.9% yield and RSI 68; financials are the explicit beneficiary of the de-escalation/'improved economic growth' rotation, and Q2 earnings land 7/14 (est EPS 1.10). [ASSUMED] I cut JPM and GE but keep BAC small (0.08) as a single value/financial diversifier so the book isn't purely semis+pharma. I already hold it (-1.6%).

reflection.looking_back: On 6/29 I set targets MU 0.11 / BAC 0.10 / JPM 0.10 / TSM 0.08 / GE 0.07 / LLY 0.07 (~53% invested, ~47% cash) and began rotating out of the UNH defensive hedge toward semis. The book returned +0.2% while SPY ran +1.6% — a -1.4pt alpha miss. All-time the book is -10.3% vs SPY -1.3% (-9.0pts behind), annualized Sharpe -4.74 over 12 noisy sessions.
worked: Holding the MU core paid: it's +1.5% vs entry and the cheapest semis P/E (25.9); refusing to panic-sell the 6/26 -7.5% scare was the right call [DATA].

The instinct to rotate toward semis was directionally correct — semis are the confirmed leadership (6/29 Nasdaq-100 +2.3%; Astera +16%, KLA +12%) and my RS screen now ranks MRVL/MU/TSM at the top of the universe [DATA].
missed: Too cautious, too slow: at ~47% cash with a heavy financials/industrials value sleeve, the book made only +0.2% on a +1.6% SPY day — I under-deployed into the leadership I had already identified [DATA].
The BAC/JPM/GE value tilt lagged the semis/tech that actually led the tape; carrying three correlated cyclicals diluted the semis call I was trying to make [DATA].
reflection.adjustment: Commit to the leadership instead of dabbling: exit JPM and GE, finish cutting the UNH hedge, keep only one cheap financial (BAC 0.08), and concentrate the rotation into a semis-led core (MU/MRVL/TSM ~44%) plus an LLY growth hedge — raising deployment from ~53% to ~62%, with cash (not a defensive stock) as the sole downside hedge.

swarm.note: Live swarm — 150 fish, each a unique trader profile shown its own random ~20-name slice of the watchlist (with recent headlines), voted via OpenRouter. Independent-voter election; dissenters (ringed) chose CASH.

live.note: Connected to the Agentic cash account •••• — currently unfunded ($0). Deposit into this specific account to trade the real $100. No agent orders placed yet.

rules.momentum_breakout: Buy a 20-day-high breakout confirmed by above-average volume; exit when price closes below its 20-day SMA. Few big winners, many small losers.
rules.mean_reversion: Buy when RSI(14) < 30 (oversold); sell when RSI(14) > 70 (overbought). Trades less, wins more often, smaller edge.
rules.blended_momo_rsi: Momentum breakout, but skip entries already overbought (RSI ≥ 70); exit on trend break or RSI > 75. Avoids chasing extended moves.
rules.deep_research_analyst: Holds the analyst's target weights; rebalanced each tick from a fresh research report (web + Robinhood data).
rules.llm_voters: Allocates across the swarm's top vote-getters (weight ∝ vote share, capped at 60%); rebalanced each tick.
rules.mirofish_real: Real-MiroFish: persona agents with memory interact over rounds, each ranking its best ideas; the panel's rank-weighted consensus (top 5 names, weight ∝ Borda points) rebalanced each tick.
rules.congress_mirror: Mirrors members of Congress from the kadoa STOCK Act disclosure mirror: follows every filer with a real track record and buys the in-universe names they disclosed purchasing within 120 days, weighting each name by how many distinct members bought it (consensus = conviction). Traded on the disclosure date — weeks after their actual fill.

sources.name: Standings, curves, decision trail (LIVE)
sources.detail: The forward paper test — every competitor trades a $100 book the same way, advanced one trading day per tick (rule strategies via tick.py / bot/engine.py; agents via run_agents.py / bot/paper.py), 5 bps slippage, −15% stop. Young — it grows one session per tick.

sources.name: Backtest reference
sources.detail: A separate Dec–Jun walk-forward backtest (2026-01-02–2026-06-30, in-sample, mostly-bull) is shown inside each rule strategy's detail for context only — it is NOT the live board.

sources.name: Research analyst
sources.detail: Agent-driven on the Claude Code plan: web_search + Robinhood data → one report (state/analyst.json) with target weights per tick. Sources link from each evidence row.

sources.name: Swarm vote
sources.detail: Independent-voter election: 150 unique-profile cheap models (DeepSeek/Gemini/Llama/Haiku via OpenRouter) each read one shared briefing and return a single ballot; tallied in bot/swarm.py. No interaction between fish.

sources.name: Real-MiroFish swarm
sources.detail: Social-simulation swarm (bot/mirofish.py): persona agents with memory interact over multiple rounds — each re-ranks its best ideas seeing its own prior view and its neighbours' latest — so a consensus forms via peer influence (the opposite of the independent swarm). The panel's rank-weighted (Borda) consensus becomes a multi-name book (top 5 names, weight ∝ points). Tiered cost (cheap/default/qwen); seeded from the briefing + optional world-events news.

sources.name: Live account
sources.detail: Robinhood MCP, Agentic cash account ••••, via get_portfolio / get_equity_positions / get_equity_orders.

sources.name: Display scale
sources.detail: The real paper books are $100 each; every dollar and share figure on this site is shown ×100 (a $10,000 book) so holdings read in whole shares and dollars. Per-share prices and percentages are unscaled.
